title: apium montanum subsp. ranunculifolium
intro: Apium montanum subsp. ranunculifolium, also known as wild celery, is a subspecies of wild celery that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Apium montanum subsp. ranunculifolium include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by combining the leaves and roots with hot water or a solvent.
